MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-26-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Craig P. Ramsdell, Esq., GAROFOLO & RAMSDELL, LLP 415-981-8500 cramsdell@garofololaw.com Attorneys for Plaintiff Michael Engmann
Russell Taylor, Esq., DONAHUE FITZGERALD LLP (925) 746-7770 rtaylor@donahue.com Attorneys for Defendant CASCADE SETTLEMENT SERVICES, LLC

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CASCADE SETTLEMENT SERVICES, LLC MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND

Defendant Cascade Settlement Services, LLC's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Plaintiff never properly effectuated service of process on defendant and thus the default and the default judgment are vacated per CCP 473(d).

Defendant's motion for relief per CCP 473(d) is timely. (California Capital Insurance Co. v. Hoehn (2024) DJDAR 10673 [void default/default judgment may be set aside at any time].)

Plaintiff purportedly served defendant on May 24, 2021 by serving CT Corp., defendant's asserted agent for service of process. (Proof of Service February 24, 2022.) But the evidence establishes that CT Corp. was the agent for service of process for a separate entity ("Cascade Settlement Services Claim Purchase Fund II LLC") and defendant's agent for service was InCorp Services, Inc. (Chilcott Decl., pars. 3-4.) Since plaintiff never properly served defendant with a copy of the summons and complaint, the default and default judgment are void.

The parties did not clearly stipulate to proper service. During settlement discussions in March 2022, the parties discussed the propriety of plaintiff's service. (Ramsdell Decl., Ex. E [March 18, 2022 e-mail].) Defendant's counsel stated: "In any event if this is not resolved, my client informs me that Cascade is willing to stipulate service as of April 11." In response, plaintiff 's counsel stated: "we agree regarding service to avoid a dispute." This cryptic exchange does not manifest a clear intent to make a general appearance or to stipulate to effective service. Defendant's alleged acceptance of service was conditional and it is unclear exactly what was to be "resolved" and if the conditions were met. Plaintiff's conduct subsequent to this exchange further supports defendant's position that there was no stipulation for service. E-mail exchanges between the parties in 2024 show that service remained a disputed issue and plaintiff never raised the purported stipulation. (Jolley Reply Decl., Ex. O).) Plaintiff's conduct is probative regarding the meaning of the 2022 e-mails. (See Southern Cal. Edison Co. v. Superior Court (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 839, 851 ["The rule is well settled that in construing the terms of a contract the construction given it by the acts and conduct of the parties with knowledge of its terms, and before any controversy has arisen as to its meaning, is admissible on the issue of the parties' intent."].) Moreover, the policy to hear cases on the merits favors granting relief to defendant. (McClain v. Kissler (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 399, 413.)

Plaintiff fails to show that defendant generally appeared in this action and having actual notice of the action does not confer jurisdiction. (Kremerman v. White (2021) 71 Cal.App.5th 358, 371 ["[C]ompliance with the statutory procedures for service of process is essential to establish personal jurisdiction. [Citation.] Thus, a default judgment entered against a defendant who was not served with a summons in the manner prescribed by statute is void."].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)

MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar today for motion for Default judgment hearing as to Defendant Cascade Settlement Service, A Delaware Limited liability company.

Dept.505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:17:22 a.m., pausing at 10:05:52 a.m., reconvening at 10:34:41 and concluding at 10:48:10 a.m.(D505)

Craig Ramsdell, Esq. representing Plaintiff Michael Engmann, via Zoom Video Conference.
Plaintiff Michael Engmann, was placed under oath via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Cascade Settlement Service, A Delaware Limited liability company not appearing.

Court commences.

Court reads into the record the documents received and reviewed by the court.

Counsel provide offer of proof and updates to support the prayer of relief.

Court recites on the record exhibits submitted:
Exhibit A: series of electronic mail correspondence between Plaintiff's and Defendant's counsel.
Exhibit B: US Court of Appeals for the 2nd Circuit affirming the settlement in the class action litigation.
Exhibit C: current status of the litigation, claiming deadline.

Court and counsel confer as to the status of the claims process.

As instructed by the Court, clerk administers the witness oath to counsel.

Court confirms with counsel that declarations filed are true and correct.

Court recites the exhibits in the declaration of the Plaintiff as follows:
Exhibit A: a true and correct copy of the Investment letter dated 10-12-2012.
Exhibit B: a true and correct copy of the Asset Purchase agreement between Defendant and Pool Corporation dated 9-28-2012.
Exhibit C: electronic mail correspondence between Plaintiff and Defendant.
Exhibit D: electronic mail correspondence between plaintiff and Defendant dated 4-29-2020, 12-31-2020, 01-04-2021, 01-05-2021.
Exhibit E: Letter agreements for other purchases.

Court confirms with Plaintiff that declaration made is true and correct.

The Court admits all exhibits presented into evidence.

The matter of the ambiguity of the agreements is discussed and argued.

Court reviews the proposed judgment and compares to the claims of action in the complaint.

Court finds scrivener's errors as to the date the agreement in multiple areas of the complaint.

Court reviews the entry of default filed April 11, 2023 and finds errors.

Counsel request for a partial judgment in favor of the Plaintiff.

Brief recess is taken at 10:05:52 a.m.

Back on the record at 10:34:41 a.m., all are present via Zoom Video Conference.

Court asks counsel how to proceed, to cure errors or waive the right to a full judgment.

The matter is discussed and argued.

Court continues the matter to January 17, 2024, at 9:00a.m. in Department 505. Plaintiff to cure errors by filing the proper documents.

Court is adjourned.

MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment hearing against Defendant Cascade Settlement Services.

Dept. 505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:26:31 a.m.; pausing at 10:00:30 a.m.; commencing at 10:00:55 a.m., pausing at 10:01:23 a.m. and concluding at 10:07:03 a.m.(D505)

Craig Ramsdell, Esq. specially appearing for Plaintiff Michael Engmann.
Plaintiff Michael Engmann was placed under oath and testified.
Defendant Cascade Settlement Services not appearing.

Plaintiff's complaint for declaratory and injunctive relief for breach of contract was filed April 22,2021.
Proof of personal service as to Defendant served on May 24, 2021.
Default entered as to Defendant filed on April 11, 2023.
Proof of service of notice of Default Judgment motion filed August 16, 2023.

Counsel for Plaintiff requests that Defendant Does 1-10 be dismissed.

The Court orally grants the request to dismiss Defendant Does 1-10, Counsel is to file the dismissal paperwork.

The Plaintiff submits the following exhibit/s for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Declaration of Michael Engmann
Exhibit 2: Agreement of the First Claim
Exhibit 3: Agreement of the Second Claim

The Court moves these exhibits and are admitted into evidence.

The Court examines the Plaintiff.

The Plaintiff confirms with the Court that the exhibits presented and the statements made by Counsel are true and correct.

The Court reviews the proposed judgment and finds that it lacks specificity for enforcement.

The Court confirms that the witness is credible, and that the parties refer to electronic mail which is not part of the exhibits.

The Court continues this matter to allow for Plaintiff to file additional documents to support specificity to the proposed judgment and provided the date of the final judgment of the originating case, to November 29, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 505.
